articles/extension-points.html.title: Extension points | CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference
articles/extension-points.html.summary: Extension points This guide describes the supported ways to extend, customize, and integrate the Cosmos DB to Azure SQL migration assessment engine from your own .NET code. Everything here uses the public API surface documented in the API Reference. Note The command-line front end (Cli), the dependency-injection composition root (DependencyInjection), and the run orchestrator (Orchestration) are internal implementation details. Public extensibility is intended through direct use of the public services, the public options types, and IDataFactoryPipelineGenerator in caller-owned composition code — the bundled CLI is not itself a plug-in host. 1. Compose the public services in your own host Every high-level service has a public constructor that takes only IConfiguration and an ILogger<T>, so you can register them in your own dependency-injection container (or construct them directly) and call them from any host — a worker service, an ASP.NET Core endpoint, or a custom CLI: using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection; using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging; var services = new ServiceCollection(); services.AddSingleton<IConfiguration>(configuration); services.AddLogging(b => b.AddConsole()); // Register only the engine pieces you need. services.AddSingleton<CosmosDbAnalysisService>(); services.AddSingleton<SqlMigrationAssessmentService>(); services.AddSingleton<ReportGenerationService>(); using var provider = services.BuildServiceProvider(); var cosmos = provider.GetRequiredService<CosmosDbAnalysisService>(); This is the primary integration seam: the services are independent and can be used piecemeal — for example, running only the analysis stage and feeding the resulting CosmosDbAnalysis into your own tooling. Note Composition lets you choose which services to use and how to host them, but some dependencies are constructed internally rather than injected: for instance CosmosDbAnalysisService builds its own CosmosClient from configuration and DefaultAzureCredential, so DI registration does not let you substitute a custom Cosmos client, credential, or retry policy. 2. Replace Azure Data Factory generation: IDataFactoryPipelineGenerator Data Factory artifact generation is abstracted behind the IDataFactoryPipelineGenerator contract. Provide your own implementation to change the generated topology entirely (for example, to emit Synapse pipelines or a bespoke ARM layout) while keeping the rest of the pipeline unchanged: public sealed class MyPipelineGenerator : IDataFactoryPipelineGenerator { public Task<DataFactoryGenerationResult> GenerateAsync( AssessmentResult assessment, string outputDirectory, DataFactoryGenerationOptions? options = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) { // ...emit your own artifacts under outputDirectory... return Task.FromResult(new DataFactoryGenerationResult()); } } // Register your implementation instead of the built-in service: services.AddSingleton<IDataFactoryPipelineGenerator, MyPipelineGenerator>(); Consumers should depend on the interface (IDataFactoryPipelineGenerator) rather than the concrete DataFactoryPipelineGenerationService so the implementation can be swapped without code changes. This applies in your own host or integration layer; the bundled CLI wires the built-in generator through its internal composition root and does not discover replacements via DI. 3. Tune the built-in Data Factory generator If the default generator's topology is fine but you want to adjust individual artifacts, DataFactoryPipelineGenerationService accepts an optional, pre-configured instance of each of its sub-builders through its constructor (every parameter defaults to null, which selects the standard builder): Builder Responsibility LinkedServiceBuilder Cosmos / Azure SQL / Key Vault linked services DatasetBuilder Source Cosmos and sink Azure SQL datasets CopyActivityBuilder Per-mapping Copy activities FailureNotificationBuilder Web + Fail notification pairs ValidationActivityBuilder Row-count validation triplets ArmTemplateBuilder Deployable ARM template IncrementalCopyActivityBuilder Incremental watermark activity group WatermarkSchemaBuilder Watermark DDL / SQL scripts DiagnosticSettingsTemplateBuilder Diagnostic-settings ARM template var generator = new DataFactoryPipelineGenerationService( logger, copyActivityBuilder: new CopyActivityBuilder()); // supply a pre-configured builder The built-in generator exposes construction-time customization seams for its sealed helper builders: supply a pre-configured builder instance to adjust the corresponding artifacts. The builders are sealed, so they are not polymorphic extension points — for behavioral changes beyond what those builders and the options below support, implement IDataFactoryPipelineGenerator (section 2). 4. Adjust behavior with options objects Several entry points take an options object that toggles behavior without requiring a custom implementation: DataFactoryGenerationOptions — settings for the built-in ADF generator, in areas such as full vs. incremental load, per-pipeline activity chunking, and validation/monitoring behavior. IncrementalCopyOptions — settings for incremental copy, such as watermark handling and safety lag, used when incremental load is enabled. SqlProjectOptions — SQL Database Project generation settings such as the project name and output path. var result = await generator.GenerateAsync( assessment, outputDirectory: "out", options: new DataFactoryGenerationOptions { // enable incremental load, set chunking, etc. }); 5. Drive behavior through configuration Because the services read their settings from IConfiguration, much of the engine can be tuned purely through appsettings.json / environment variables — no code changes: Key Used by Purpose CosmosDb:AccountEndpoint analysis / data-quality services Cosmos account to assess CosmosDb:DatabaseName CosmosDbAnalysisService Default database when none is passed AzureMonitor:WorkspaceId CosmosDbAnalysisService Enables Log Analytics performance metrics DataFactory:NetworkBandwidthMbps, DataFactory:SourceRegion, DataFactory:TargetRegion DataFactoryEstimateService Tunes migration time/cost estimates Reporting:OutputDirectory ReportGenerationService Default report output folder 6. Consume the streaming analysis API For large accounts, CosmosDbAnalysisService exposes an async-streaming overload so you can build your own pipeline that consumes each container's analysis as it is produced — emitting progress, persisting incrementally, or fanning out — using await foreach: await foreach (ContainerAnalysis container in cosmos.AnalyzeContainersStreamingAsync("OrdersDb", cancellationToken)) { // your custom per-container handling } 7. Control authentication The Azure-facing services authenticate with DefaultAzureCredential, so authentication follows the Azure Identity default credential chain for the runtime environment — managed identity, the Azure CLI, environment variables, or interactive sign-in, depending on what is available. Environment-based credentials can be supplied with the standard AZURE_* variables in your host rather than in code.

index.html.title: CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference | CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference
index.html.summary: CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference Welcome to the developer API reference for the Cosmos DB to Azure SQL Migration Assessment Tool — a .NET 8 console application that analyzes an Azure Cosmos DB account and produces migration assessments, indexing recommendations, Azure Data Factory pipeline artifacts, and Excel/Word reports for Azure SQL targets. This site documents the tool's public API surface so you can extend, embed, or integrate the assessment engine in your own automation. For installation and end-user usage, see the project README and the docs/ folder. Tip Browse the full type listing under API Reference in the top navigation. Public namespaces Namespace What lives here CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services The analysis engine — the primary integration entry points: CosmosDbAnalysisService, SqlMigrationAssessmentService, DataQualityAnalysisService, DataFactoryEstimateService, SqlProjectGenerationService, SqlProjectIntegrationService, and ValidationScriptGeneratorService. CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory Azure Data Factory pipeline / ARM-template generation: DataFactoryPipelineGenerationService, the IDataFactoryPipelineGenerator contract, and the builder/escaper helpers that assemble the generated artifacts. CosmosToSqlAssessment.Reporting ReportGenerationService — Excel and Word report rendering. CosmosToSqlAssessment.SqlProject SQL database project (.sqlproj) generation. CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models Data-model types passed across the pipeline — Cosmos analysis results, SQL assessment output, data-quality findings, and Data Factory artifact models (CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.DataFactory). Note The command-line front end (Cli), the dependency-injection composition root (DependencyInjection), and the run orchestrator (Orchestration) are internal implementation details — exposed to the test project via InternalsVisibleTo — and are intentionally not part of the supported public API. Integrators compose the public services above directly. Integration entry points The public services are designed to be used individually. Each is registered in the application's dependency-injection container, but they can equally be constructed directly and called from your own host: CosmosDbAnalysisService analyzes an account/database, SqlMigrationAssessmentService turns that analysis into Azure SQL recommendations, DataFactoryPipelineGenerationService emits the migration pipeline artifacts, and ReportGenerationService renders the result to Excel/Word. Services that walk potentially large Cosmos datasets expose async-streaming signatures (IAsyncEnumerable<T>), so results are produced lazily and the working set stays bounded regardless of collection size. Each streaming method accepts a CancellationToken. See the API Reference for full type, member, parameter, and return documentation. Guides Extension points — how to extend, customize, and integrate the assessment engine (custom Data Factory generation, options, configuration, streaming, and authentication seams).

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.title: Enum AgentExecutionMode | CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference
reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.summary: Enum AgentExecutionMode Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Strategy the AgentOrchestrator uses to schedule a set of agents. public enum AgentExecutionMode Fields Sequential = 0 Run agents one at a time in dependency order. Deterministic and easiest to debug. Parallel = 1 Run agents whose dependencies are already satisfied concurrently, scheduling more as dependencies complete. Maximizes throughput for independent work. Conditional = 2 Run agents in dependency order but skip any agent whose required upstream outputs are missing (for example because an upstream agent failed), rather than failing the whole run. Remarks The mode selects a scheduling policy; actual ordering and gating are derived from each agent's Dependencies so the orchestrator never hardcodes per-agent rules.

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.summary: Class AgentOrchestrationOptions Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Options that control how the AgentOrchestrator schedules and bounds an agentic run. public sealed record AgentOrchestrationOptions : IEquatable<AgentOrchestrationOptions> Inheritance object AgentOrchestrationOptions Implements IEquatable<AgentOrchestrationOptions> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Default A shared default instance using sequential scheduling and no timeout. public static AgentOrchestrationOptions Default { get; } Property Value AgentOrchestrationOptions Mode How producer agents are scheduled. Defaults to Sequential, which reproduces the single-pass order (Cosmos → SQL → data quality → Data Factory). public AgentExecutionMode Mode { get; init; } Property Value AgentExecutionMode PerAgentTimeout Optional per-agent timeout. Cooperative: it cancels the token passed to each agent, so it only bounds agents (and the services they wrap) that observe cancellation. A timed-out agent is recorded as a failed result and the run continues. null (default) means no timeout. public TimeSpan? PerAgentTimeout { get; init; } Property Value TimeSpan?

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.summary: Class AgentOrchestrationResult Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll The immutable outcome of an AgentOrchestrator run. It exposes point-in-time snapshots only; the live, mutable SharedAssessmentContext is intentionally not surfaced so callers cannot retroactively change the projected result. public sealed record AgentOrchestrationResult : IEquatable<AgentOrchestrationResult> Inheritance object AgentOrchestrationResult Implements IEquatable<AgentOrchestrationResult> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Consumption contract: AssessmentResult is a best-effort projection that is always non-null but is only safe to drive downstream report / SQL-project / Data Factory generation when IsAcceptable is true. When IsAcceptable is false (a required output is missing or outputs are inconsistent), the result may contain empty defaults and consumers should surface the failure instead of generating artifacts. Derived from IsAcceptable; false when no report was produced. public bool IsAcceptable { get; init; } Property Value bool Messages A snapshot of every message logged during the run, in order. public IReadOnlyList<AgentMessage> Messages { get; init; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<AgentMessage> Mode The execution mode the run was scheduled with. public AgentExecutionMode Mode { get; init; } Property Value AgentExecutionMode Validation The validator's verdict, or null if validation did not produce a report. public ValidationReport? reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.title: Class AgentOrchestrator | CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference
reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.summary: Class AgentOrchestrator Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Coordinates the assessment agents over a single SharedAssessmentContext, scheduling them according to their declared Dependencies and the requested AgentExecutionMode, isolating per-agent failures, and finishing with the validation pass. public sealed class AgentOrchestrator Inheritance object AgentOrchestrator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This is the stable public entry point that downstream agentic features build on. Producers (every agent except the validator) run first; the validator runs last so it observes every result. The Data Factory estimate is produced by an internal agent and therefore schedules uniformly through the dependency graph. The orchestrator validates the agent graph up front (see the constructor) and never throws for a recoverable agent failure — a failed or timed-out agent is recorded and the run continues, mirroring the single-pass pipeline's tolerance while still flagging missing required outputs through the validator. Constructors AgentOrchestrator(IEnumerable<IAssessmentAgent>, ILogger<AgentOrchestrator>) Creates a new AgentOrchestrator over the supplied agents. public AgentOrchestrator(IEnumerable<IAssessmentAgent> agents, ILogger<AgentOrchestrator> logger) Parameters agents IEnumerable<IAssessmentAgent> The agents to coordinate. Exactly one agent must own each of the required roles (CosmosAnalysis, SqlPlanning, DataFactoryEstimation, Validation); data quality is optional. No two agents may share a role or a name, and every dependency role must be produced by a registered agent. logger ILogger<AgentOrchestrator> Logger for diagnostic output. Exceptions ArgumentNullException If agents or logger is null. InvalidOperationException If the agent graph violates the invariants above. Methods RunAsync(string, string, AgentOrchestrationOptions?, CancellationToken) Runs the agentic assessment for a single database and returns an immutable result. public Task<AgentOrchestrationResult> RunAsync(string databaseName, string cosmosAccountName, AgentOrchestrationOptions? options = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters databaseName string The Cosmos DB database to assess. cosmosAccountName string Friendly name of the Cosmos DB account (used in the projected result). options AgentOrchestrationOptions Scheduling options; defaults to sequential with no timeout. cancellationToken CancellationToken Token used to cancel the whole run (propagates; does not become a failed result). Returns Task<AgentOrchestrationResult> The immutable AgentOrchestrationResult for the run.

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.summary: Class AgentResult Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Immutable record of how a single agent run finished. Recorded on the SharedAssessmentContext so the orchestrator can isolate failures and the ValidatorAgent can reason about completeness. public sealed record AgentResult : IEquatable<AgentResult> Inheritance object AgentResult Implements IEquatable<AgentResult> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Contract: a Succeeded result guarantees the agent's required outputs were committed to the context. Failed and Skipped make no such guarantee — consumers must treat the corresponding outputs as possibly absent. Properties AgentName The Name of the agent this result describes. public required string AgentName { get; init; } Property Value string Duration Wall-clock duration of the run. Zero for skipped agents. public TimeSpan Duration { get; init; } Property Value TimeSpan Error The failure message when Status is Failed, or the skip reason when Skipped. null on success. public string? Error { get; init; } Property Value string Role The domain role the agent owns. public required AgentRole Role { get; init; } Property Value AgentRole Status The terminal status of the run. public required AgentRunStatus Status { get; init; } Property Value AgentRunStatus Methods Failed(string, AgentRole, string, TimeSpan) Creates a failed result. public static AgentResult Failed(string agentName, AgentRole role, string error, TimeSpan duration) Parameters agentName string The agent's name. role AgentRole The agent's role. error string The failure message. duration TimeSpan Wall-clock duration before failure. Returns AgentResult A failed AgentResult. Skipped(string, AgentRole, string) Creates a skipped result. public static AgentResult Skipped(string agentName, AgentRole role, string reason) Parameters agentName string The agent's name. role AgentRole The agent's role. reason string Why the agent was skipped (e.g. an unmet dependency). Returns AgentResult A skipped AgentResult. Succeeded(string, AgentRole, TimeSpan) Creates a successful result. public static AgentResult Succeeded(string agentName, AgentRole role, TimeSpan duration) Parameters agentName string The agent's name. role AgentRole The agent's role. duration TimeSpan Wall-clock duration of the run. Returns AgentResult A succeeded AgentResult.

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.title: Enum AgentRole | CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference
reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.summary: Enum AgentRole Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Identifies the assessment domain an IAssessmentAgent owns and the kind of output it contributes to a SharedAssessmentContext. public enum AgentRole Fields CosmosAnalysis = 0 Analysis of the source Cosmos DB account, databases, and containers. SqlPlanning = 1 Azure SQL platform recommendation and migration planning. DataQuality = 2 Pre-migration data-quality analysis of the source documents. DataFactoryEstimation = 3 Azure Data Factory migration estimate derived from the Cosmos analysis and SQL plan. Produced by the orchestrator as a derived step rather than by a domain agent. Validation = 4 Cross-checking and completeness validation of other agents' outputs. Orchestration = 5 Coordination of the agent run itself (used by the orchestrator). Remarks Roles let the orchestrator and the validator reason about which domain produced a given result without depending on concrete agent types. Adding new values is a source-compatible change, so the enum can grow as later epics introduce more agents.

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.title: Enum AgentRunStatus | CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference
reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.summary: Enum AgentRunStatus Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Terminal outcome of a single IAssessmentAgent run. public enum AgentRunStatus Fields Succeeded = 0 The agent completed and committed all of its required outputs to the context. Failed = 1 The agent threw or otherwise failed; its outputs may be absent or partial. Skipped = 2 The agent did not run because a precondition (e.g. a dependency) was not met.

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.title: Class AssessmentAgentBase | CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference
reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.A.summary: Class AssessmentAgentBase Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Base class for assessment agents that centralises run timing, structured logging, and the failure-isolation contract so concrete agents only implement their domain logic. public abstract class AssessmentAgentBase : IAssessmentAgent Inheritance object AssessmentAgentBase Implements IAssessmentAgent Derived CosmosAnalyzerAgent DataQualityAgent SqlPlannerAgent ValidatorAgent Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks RunAsync(ISharedAssessmentContext, CancellationToken) never throws for a recoverable agent error: any exception (other than cancellation) is converted into an Failed(string, AgentRole, string, TimeSpan) result and recorded on the context, so one agent's failure cannot abort the rest of the run. Cancellation convention. OperationCanceledException is deliberately re-thrown rather than recorded, so a host-level cancellation (Ctrl+C) can propagate and map to the process's exit-code-130 handling. A per-agent timeout is therefore the orchestrator's responsibility: it should run each agent under a linked CancellationTokenSource and catch OperationCanceledException when (!globalToken.IsCancellationRequested) to record a failed/skipped timeout result while still letting global cancellation bubble up. Concrete agents should commit their domain output (via the context's Set* methods) as the last meaningful action in ExecuteAsync(ISharedAssessmentContext, CancellationToken), so a later failure never leaves the context partially populated. Properties Dependencies The roles whose outputs this agent requires before it can run. The orchestrator uses this to order sequential runs, gate conditional runs, and schedule parallel runs. An empty collection means the agent has no upstream dependencies. public virtual IReadOnlyCollection<AgentRole> Dependencies { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyCollection<AgentRole> Name A stable, unique, human-readable identifier for this agent (e.g. CosmosAnalyzer). public abstract string Name { get; } Property Value string Role The assessment domain this agent owns and contributes output for. public abstract AgentRole Role { get; } Property Value AgentRole Methods ExecuteAsync(ISharedAssessmentContext, CancellationToken) Performs the agent's domain work. Implementations should compute locally and commit their single domain output to context as the final action. Throwing here is converted into a failed result by RunAsync(ISharedAssessmentContext, CancellationToken); OperationCanceledException propagates. protected abstract Task ExecuteAsync(ISharedAssessmentContext context, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters context ISharedAssessmentContext The shared blackboard to read upstream outputs from and write this agent's output to. cancellationToken CancellationToken Token used to cancel the run. Returns Task A task that completes when the agent's work is done. GetSkipReason(ISharedAssessmentContext) Returns a non-null reason to skip this agent (e.g. a required upstream output is missing), or null to run normally. Evaluated before ExecuteAsync(ISharedAssessmentContext, CancellationToken). The default never skips. protected virtual string? GetSkipReason(ISharedAssessmentContext context) Parameters context ISharedAssessmentContext The shared context, used to inspect upstream outputs. Returns string A skip reason, or null to proceed. RunAsync(ISharedAssessmentContext, CancellationToken) Executes the agent against the shared context and returns its terminal result. Implementations should catch their own recoverable errors and report them through the returned AgentResult rather than throwing. public Task<AgentResult> RunAsync(ISharedAssessmentContext context, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters context ISharedAssessmentContext The shared blackboard to read upstream outputs from and write this agent's output to. cancellationToken CancellationToken Token used to cancel the run. Returns Task<AgentResult> The terminal AgentResult describing how the run finished.

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.I.title: Interface IAssessmentAgent | CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference
reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.I.summary: Interface IAssessmentAgent Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll A single-responsibility participant in an agentic assessment run. Each agent owns one AgentRole, reads any upstream outputs it needs from the shared context, performs its work, and commits its own output back to the context. public interface IAssessmentAgent Remarks This is the stable abstraction the orchestrator (#215) and later epics (#132/#133/#69) compose against. Implementations must: be safe to construct via dependency injection and to run independently in isolation; never throw for a recoverable condition — return a Failed(string, AgentRole, string, TimeSpan) or Skipped(string, AgentRole, string) result and log a message instead, so a single agent's problem does not abort the whole run; commit their domain output exactly once via the context's Set* methods on success. Properties Dependencies The roles whose outputs this agent requires before it can run. The orchestrator uses this to order sequential runs, gate conditional runs, and schedule parallel runs. An empty collection means the agent has no upstream dependencies. IReadOnlyCollection<AgentRole> Dependencies { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyCollection<AgentRole> Name A stable, unique, human-readable identifier for this agent (e.g. CosmosAnalyzer). string Name { get; } Property Value string Role The assessment domain this agent owns and contributes output for. AgentRole Role { get; } Property Value AgentRole Methods RunAsync(ISharedAssessmentContext, CancellationToken) Executes the agent against the shared context and returns its terminal result. Implementations should catch their own recoverable errors and report them through the returned AgentResult rather than throwing. Task<AgentResult> RunAsync(ISharedAssessmentContext context, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters context ISharedAssessmentContext The shared blackboard to read upstream outputs from and write this agent's output to. cancellationToken CancellationToken Token used to cancel the run. Returns Task<AgentResult> The terminal AgentResult describing how the run finished.

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.V.title: Enum ValidationFindingCategory | CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference
reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.V.summary: Enum ValidationFindingCategory Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Classifies a ValidationFinding by the kind of problem it represents, so consumers can reason about which findings affect run completeness versus cross-output consistency versus pure diagnostics. public enum ValidationFindingCategory Fields Completeness = 0 A required domain output is missing. Completeness findings drive IsComplete. Consistency = 1 Two or more agent outputs disagree (e.g. a Cosmos container has no SQL mapping). An Error consistency finding makes IsConsistent false. Diagnostic = 2 Informational or non-fatal observation (e.g. an optional agent was skipped or failed). Diagnostic findings never affect the report's IsAcceptable verdict.

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.V.summary: Class ValidatorAgent Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Agent that cross-checks the outputs produced by the other agents and emits a typed ValidationReport describing whether the run is complete and internally consistent. public sealed class ValidatorAgent : AssessmentAgentBase, IAssessmentAgent Inheritance object AssessmentAgentBase ValidatorAgent Implements IAssessmentAgent Inherited Members AssessmentAgentBase.RunAsync(ISharedAssessmentContext, CancellationToken) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The validator never skips: it always runs so it can flag whatever is missing, and is scheduled last by the orchestrator (after the optional data-quality agent and the derived Data Factory step). Its Dependencies list only the agent-backed required producers so a dependency-ordering scheduler places it after them; it does not depend on the optional data-quality output (which may legitimately never be produced). Finding a problem is a successful validation, not a failure: the agent records Succeeded as long as it produced a report, even when that report is not acceptable. Only a genuine execution fault makes the agent fail — and even then the validator hardens its cross-checks so a malformed upstream output becomes a finding rather than a crash, ensuring a report is (almost) always emitted. The orchestrator/CLI derive the process exit code from IsAcceptable. reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents.h.summary: Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Agents Classes AgentMessage An immutable, timestamped log entry produced by an agent and appended to the shared Messages blackboard. AgentOrchestrationOptions Options that control how the AgentOrchestrator schedules and bounds an agentic run. AgentOrchestrationResult The immutable outcome of an AgentOrchestrator run. It exposes point-in-time snapshots only; the live, mutable SharedAssessmentContext is intentionally not surfaced so callers cannot retroactively change the projected result. AgentOrchestrator Coordinates the assessment agents over a single SharedAssessmentContext, scheduling them according to their declared Dependencies and the requested AgentExecutionMode, isolating per-agent failures, and finishing with the validation pass. AgentResult Immutable record of how a single agent run finished. Recorded on the SharedAssessmentContext so the orchestrator can isolate failures and the ValidatorAgent can reason about completeness. AssessmentAgentBase Base class for assessment agents that centralises run timing, structured logging, and the failure-isolation contract so concrete agents only implement their domain logic. CosmosAnalyzerAgent Agent that owns the Cosmos DB analysis domain. It wraps the existing CosmosDbAnalysisService (whose signatures are intentionally unchanged) and commits the resulting CosmosDbAnalysis to the shared context for downstream agents. DataQualityAgent Agent that owns the pre-migration data-quality domain. It wraps the existing DataQualityAnalysisService (unchanged) and commits the resulting DataQualityAnalysis to the shared context. SharedAssessmentContext Thread-safe blackboard implementation of ISharedAssessmentContext. A single private lock guards every piece of mutable state (the four domain outputs, the message log, and the result log) so concurrently scheduled agents observe a consistent view. SqlPlannerAgent Agent that owns the Azure SQL migration-planning domain. It wraps the existing SqlMigrationAssessmentService (unchanged) and commits the resulting SqlMigrationAssessment to the shared context. ValidationFinding A single observation produced by the ValidatorAgent while cross-checking agent outputs. ValidationReport The typed verdict produced by the ValidatorAgent after cross-checking the outputs of the other agents. It is the stable, structured surface downstream consumers (and the orchestrator/CLI) use to decide whether an agentic run succeeded. ValidatorAgent Agent that cross-checks the outputs produced by the other agents and emits a typed ValidationReport describing whether the run is complete and internally consistent. Interfaces IAssessmentAgent A single-responsibility participant in an agentic assessment run. Each agent owns one AgentRole, reads any upstream outputs it needs from the shared context, performs its work, and commits its own output back to the context. ISharedAssessmentContext Thread-safe shared blackboard through which assessment agents communicate. Agents read the outputs produced by upstream agents, contribute their own domain output exactly once, append log messages, and record their run results. Implemented by SharedAssessmentContext. Enums AgentExecutionMode Strategy the AgentOrchestrator uses to schedule a set of agents. AgentMessageLevel Severity of an AgentMessage recorded on a SharedAssessmentContext. AgentRole Identifies the assessment domain an IAssessmentAgent owns and the kind of output it contributes to a SharedAssessmentContext. AgentRunStatus Terminal outcome of a single IAssessmentAgent run. ValidationFindingCategory Classifies a ValidationFinding by the kind of problem it represents, so consumers can reason about which findings affect run completeness versus cross-output consistency versus pure diagnostics.

DatasetProperties Properties bag for an ADF Dataset artifact, extending PropertiesBase with the type discriminator, linked-service reference, and connector-specific type properties. DatasetResource ADF Dataset artifact. Stand-alone JSON file under ADF/Datasets/. LinkedServiceProperties Properties bag for an ADF Linked Service artifact, extending PropertiesBase with the type discriminator and connector-specific type properties such as connection strings, account endpoints, and authentication settings. LinkedServiceResource ADF Linked Service artifact. Stand-alone JSON file under ADF/LinkedServices/. PipelineActivity Pipeline activity. Activity type (e.g. Copy, ExecutePipeline) is the discriminator; activity-specific configuration lives under TypeProperties. Retry policy / on-failure / depends-on / linked-service refs are layered in by follow-on sub-issues via the extension bag. PipelineProperties Properties bag for an ADF Pipeline artifact, extending PropertiesBase with the ordered list of activities that define the pipeline's execution graph. PipelineResource ADF Pipeline artifact. Stand-alone JSON file under ADF/Pipelines/. PropertiesBase Base for the properties bag of every ADF artifact. Provides a free-form AdditionalProperties extension dictionary plus the universally applicable Annotations list. ResourceReference Reference to a sibling artifact by logical name. ADF uses this shape everywhere (datasets in inputs/outputs, linked services on datasets, pipelines in ExecutePipeline activities, etc.). The optional Parameters bag is used by callers that pass values into a parameterised dataset, linked service, or pipeline reference.

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.M.summary: Class AlertRuleOptions Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.Monitoring Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Strongly-typed options controlling the Azure Monitor alert-rule ARM templates emitted for the migration custom metrics (#223) and the orchestrating Data Factory pipelines (#70). Bound from the AzureMonitor:Alerts configuration section. public sealed class AlertRuleOptions Inheritance object AlertRuleOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The templates are deploy-time artifacts: they describe Microsoft.Insights/metricAlerts rules over the MetricNamespace custom metrics plus a Microsoft.Insights/scheduledQueryRules log alert over the ADFPipelineRun/ADFActivityRun Log Analytics tables. None of these settings trigger a live Azure call — they only shape the generated JSON. Threshold and severity defaults are conservative starting points an operator is expected to tune; every value is surfaced as an ARM template parameter default so the emitted templates remain deployable and overridable without regeneration. Fields SectionName Configuration section these options bind from. public const string SectionName = "AzureMonitor:Alerts" Field Value string Properties Enabled Whether the generated alert rules are created in the enabled state. Defaults to true so a deployed template begins evaluating immediately. public bool Enabled { get; set; } Property Value bool ErrorRateSeverity Severity (0 = critical … 4 = verbose) of the error-rate alert. Defaults to 2. public int ErrorRateSeverity { get; set; } Property Value int ErrorRateThreshold Error-rate fraction (0-1) above which the static error-rate alert fires. Defaults to 0.05. public double ErrorRateThreshold { get; set; } Property Value double ErrorSpikeMinFailingPeriods How many of ErrorSpikeNumberOfEvaluationPeriods must breach before the error-spike alert fires. Defaults to 3. public int ErrorSpikeMinFailingPeriods { get; set; } Property Value int ErrorSpikeNumberOfEvaluationPeriods Number of recent evaluation periods the dynamic error-spike alert inspects. Defaults to 4. public int ErrorSpikeNumberOfEvaluationPeriods { get; set; } Property Value int ErrorSpikeSensitivity Dynamic-threshold sensitivity for the error-spike alert: Low, Medium, or High. Defaults to Medium. public string ErrorSpikeSensitivity { get; set; } Property Value string ErrorSpikeSeverity Severity of the dynamic error-spike alert. Defaults to 2. public int ErrorSpikeSeverity { get; set; } Property Value int EvaluationFrequency ISO-8601 duration for how often the metric alerts are evaluated. Defaults to PT1M (every minute). public string EvaluationFrequency { get; set; } Property Value string IncludeLowThroughputAlert Whether to emit the low/no-throughput metric alert. Defaults to true. Note this alert fires on observed-but-zero throughput while telemetry is flowing; a truly dead pipeline that stops emitting is caught by the stalled-pipeline log alert instead. public bool IncludeLowThroughputAlert { get; set; } Property Value bool IncludeRequestUnitsThresholdAlert Whether to emit the optional Request-Units ceiling alert. Off by default. public bool IncludeRequestUnitsThresholdAlert { get; set; } Property Value bool LowThroughputSeverity Severity of the low-throughput alert. Defaults to 1. public int LowThroughputSeverity { get; set; } Property Value int MetricNamespace Custom-metric namespace the metric alerts target. Defaults to DefaultMetricNamespace so it matches the metrics published by MigrationMonitoringService. public string MetricNamespace { get; set; } Property Value string RequestUnitsSeverity Severity of the optional Request-Units alert. Defaults to 3. public int RequestUnitsSeverity { get; set; } Property Value int RequestUnitsThreshold RU/s value above which the optional Request-Units alert fires (used only when IncludeRequestUnitsThresholdAlert is true). public double RequestUnitsThreshold { get; set; } Property Value double SkipMetricValidation Emits skipMetricValidation = true on each custom-metric criterion so a template deploys before the metrics have been ingested for the first time. Defaults to true. public bool SkipMetricValidation { get; set; } Property Value bool SkipQueryValidation Emits skipQueryValidation = true on the scheduled query rule so it deploys even when the target Log Analytics tables are not yet populated. Defaults to true. public bool SkipQueryValidation { get; set; } Property Value bool StalledActivityGap KQL relative-time literal (e.g. 15m) used inside the stalled-pipeline query's ago(...) activity-gap filter. Kept distinct from StalledWindowSize because KQL uses Timespan literals, not ISO-8601 durations. public string StalledActivityGap { get; set; } Property Value string StalledPipelineSeverity Severity of the stalled-pipeline log alert. Defaults to 1. public int StalledPipelineSeverity { get; set; } Property Value int StalledWindowSize ISO-8601 lookback window for the stalled-pipeline log alert. Defaults to PT15M, longer than WindowSize because a stalled pipeline is defined by the absence of progress over a sustained period. public string StalledWindowSize { get; set; } Property Value string WindowSize ISO-8601 lookback window each metric-alert evaluation aggregates over. Defaults to PT5M. public string WindowSize { get; set; } Property Value string

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.M.summary: Class AnomalyDetectionOptions Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Models.Monitoring Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Options for the rolling-window z-score anomaly detector (#226) applied to migration Request-Units/sec and throughput. Bound from the AzureMonitor:Anomaly section. public sealed class AnomalyDetectionOptions Inheritance object AnomalyDetectionOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The detector is a deliberately simple statistical baseline: it assumes a roughly stationary, symmetric local distribution. Bursty / heavy-tailed metrics may produce false positives; the z-score threshold and relative-change guard are the primary tuning knobs. Fields SectionName Configuration section these options bind from. public const string SectionName = "AzureMonitor:Anomaly" Field Value string Properties Enabled Whether anomaly detection is active. Defaults to true. public bool Enabled { get; set; } Property Value bool MinBaselineStdDev Floor applied to the baseline standard deviation in the z-score denominator to avoid divide-by-zero on constant series. Defaults to a tiny epsilon. public double MinBaselineStdDev { get; set; } Property Value double MinRelativeChange Minimum fractional change from the baseline mean required to flag an anomaly, guarding against flapping on near-constant baselines. Defaults to 0.25 (25%). public double MinRelativeChange { get; set; } Property Value double MinSamplesForBaseline Minimum number of prior observations required before the baseline is evaluated (warmup). Must be at least 2 (sample standard deviation is undefined for one point). Defaults to 5. public int MinSamplesForBaseline { get; set; } Property Value int SuppressLowOnTerminalStatus When true (default), low-direction anomalies are suppressed for snapshots whose status is terminal (e.g. Succeeded), so a natural ramp-down at completion does not spam warnings. public bool SuppressLowOnTerminalStatus { get; set; } Property Value bool WatchRequestUnits Whether to watch RequestUnitsPerSecond. Defaults to true. public bool WatchRequestUnits { get; set; } Property Value bool WatchThroughput Whether to watch ThroughputRowsPerSecond. Defaults to true. public bool WatchThroughput { get; set; } Property Value bool WindowSize Maximum number of recent observations retained per key/metric baseline. Defaults to 20. public int WindowSize { get; set; } Property Value int ZScoreThreshold Absolute z-score at or above which an observation is flagged. Defaults to 3.0. public double ZScoreThreshold { get; set; } Property Value double

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.title: Class ArmTemplateBuilder | CosmosDB to SQL Migration Tool — API Reference
reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.summary: Class ArmTemplateBuilder Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Builds the deployable Azure Resource Manager template that wraps every emitted ADF artifact (linked services, datasets, pipelines) under Microsoft.DataFactory/factories/{kind} resources (#146). Design notes: Serialise-then-parse input: every per-artifact properties bag is captured as a JsonObject (via JsonSerializer.SerializeToNode) so [JsonExtensionData] AdditionalProperties is already flattened — the ARM template payload matches what's written to the per-artifact JSON file byte-for-byte (modulo the targeted defaultValue rewrite below). Generic reference walker: CollectReferences(JsonNode, List<(string Kind, string Name)>) recurses through every JsonObject /JsonArray in the properties bag looking for { "type": "*Reference", "referenceName": <name> } triples. This automatically catches Dataset / LinkedService / Pipeline references plus the Key Vault LinkedServiceReference nested under typeProperties.accountKey.store on the Cosmos/SQL linked services — no per-activity-type code needed. Closed-world dependsOn: edges are only emitted for resources actually present in this template's resource set. External / operator-managed linked services (e.g. a fault-tolerance log storage LS the operator has pre-provisioned) are silently skipped here — the operator's existing deployment of those resources satisfies them at New-AzResourceGroupDeployment time. Targeted defaultValue rewrite: when a pipeline's PipelineParameterArmOverrides map is non-null, the builder walks properties.parameters.<pipelineParamName>.defaultValue and replaces the literal with [parameters('<armParamName>')]. This is the only mechanism that actually flows deployment-time parameter values into deployed ADF pipelines — without it, the parameter file would be decorative. Determinism: resources sorted by (kind, name) and dependsOn entries sorted alphabetically so the file diff is stable across runs. public sealed class ArmTemplateBuilder Inheritance object ArmTemplateBuilder Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields ApiVersion Azure Resource Manager API version targeted by every generated Microsoft.DataFactory/factories child resource. public const string ApiVersion = "2018-06-01" Field Value string DatasetKind ARM resource-kind segment for dataset artifacts; used in type and resourceId expressions. public const string DatasetKind = "datasets" Field Value string FactoriesResourceType Azure resource provider + type prefix for all ADF factory child resources. public const string FactoriesResourceType = "Microsoft.DataFactory/factories" Field Value string LinkedServiceKind ARM resource-kind segment for linked-service artifacts; used in type and resourceId expressions. public const string LinkedServiceKind = "linkedservices" Field Value string PipelineKind ARM resource-kind segment for pipeline artifacts; used in type and resourceId expressions. public const string PipelineKind = "pipelines" Field Value string Methods Build(IReadOnlyList<ArmResourceInput>, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, ArmParameterDefinition>, string) Builds the ARM template root object. public Dictionary<string, object?> Build(IReadOnlyList<ArmResourceInput> resources, IReadOnlyDictionary<string, ArmParameterDefinition> parameters, string factoryParameterName) Parameters resources IReadOnlyList<ArmResourceInput> All ADF artifacts to wrap. reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.summary: Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.DataFactory Classes ActivityPolicyBuilder Builds the ADF policy block for an activity (#143). Pure / side-effect free — returns a serialiser-ready dictionary which the orchestrator and copy-activity builder stash on AdditionalProperties. AdfJsonSerializer Single source of truth for ADF JSON serialization options. All artifact writers MUST route through this class so output is deterministic, camelCase, indented, and skips nulls — the shape Azure Data Factory imports/deploys cleanly. AdfNameRegistry Allocates collision-proof, ADF-compliant identifiers for activities, datasets, linked services, pipelines, and the on-disk file names that mirror them. ADF allows up to 260 chars for most resources but activity names are capped at 55. We use the tighter budget for everything so a single sanitizer covers all artifacts. Case-insensitive collisions get a stable 8-char hash suffix derived from the full source identity. ArmParameterDefinition ARM-template parameter declaration. ARM uses { "type": ..., "defaultValue": ..., "metadata": { "description": ... } } shape — distinct from the deployment-parameter file's { "value": ..., "metadata": ... } shape (which lives in adf-parameters.template.json). ArmResourceInput One ADF artifact slated for inclusion in the ARM template. ArmTemplateBuilder Builds the deployable Azure Resource Manager template that wraps every emitted ADF artifact (linked services, datasets, pipelines) under Microsoft.DataFactory/factories/{kind} resources (#146). Design notes: Serialise-then-parse input: every per-artifact properties bag is captured as a JsonObject (via JsonSerializer.SerializeToNode) so [JsonExtensionData] AdditionalProperties is already flattened — the ARM template payload matches what's written to the per-artifact JSON file byte-for-byte (modulo the targeted defaultValue rewrite below). Generic reference walker: CollectReferences(JsonNode, List<(string Kind, string Name)>) recurses through every JsonObject /JsonArray in the properties bag looking for { "type": "*Reference", "referenceName": <name> } triples. This automatically catches Dataset / LinkedService / Pipeline references plus the Key Vault LinkedServiceReference nested under typeProperties.accountKey.store on the Cosmos/SQL linked services — no per-activity-type code needed. Closed-world dependsOn: edges are only emitted for resources actually present in this template's resource set. External / operator-managed linked services (e.g. a fault-tolerance log storage LS the operator has pre-provisioned) are silently skipped here — the operator's existing deployment of those resources satisfies them at New-AzResourceGroupDeployment time. Targeted defaultValue rewrite: when a pipeline's PipelineParameterArmOverrides map is non-null, the builder walks properties.parameters.<pipelineParamName>.defaultValue and replaces the literal with [parameters('<armParamName>')]. This is the only mechanism that actually flows deployment-time parameter values into deployed ADF pipelines — without it, the parameter file would be decorative. Determinism: resources sorted by (kind, name) and dependsOn entries sorted alphabetically so the file diff is stable across runs. CopyActivityBuilder Translates a ContainerMapping into a fully-formed ADF Copy activity. Field mappings flagged RequiresTransformation are deliberately skipped here (annotated on the activity + surfaced as a warning) so #145 (data validation / mapping data flows) can take over without rewriting this stage. CopyActivityPolicy ADF policy shape applied to every emitted Copy activity (#143). DataFactoryGenerationOptions Behaviour toggles for the ADF generator. Defaults match the foundational #141 scope extended by #142 (parameterised, environment-agnostic artifacts); follow-on sub-issues add new properties (never replace existing). DataFactoryGenerationResult Result returned by GenerateAsync(AssessmentResult, string, DataFactoryGenerationOptions?, CancellationToken). Surfaces the list of files written and any warnings the operator should review (e.g. transformed fields skipped, child tables deferred, pipeline chunking applied). DataFactoryPipelineGenerationService Orchestrates Azure Data Factory artifact generation: composes the per-database linked service, all source / sink datasets, one Copy activity per ContainerMapping, and writes every artifact under <outputDirectory>/ADF/{LinkedServices,Datasets,Pipelines}/. Pipelines are split per source database, and any database with more than MaxActivitiesPerPipeline mappings is chunked into multiple pipeline files so ADF's per-pipeline activity limit is respected. A master orchestrator pipeline references every per-database pipeline via ExecutePipeline activities and forwards per-database parameters down. DatasetBuilder Builds ADF source / sink datasets for every Cosmos container and every target SQL table. Datasets declare parameters (e.g. databaseName, collectionName) and forward them into the linked-service reference and typeProperties so the same dataset can be reused across environments by passing parameter values from the invoking pipeline (#142). DiagnosticSettingsTemplateBuilder Emits a stand-alone, deployable ARM template containing a Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings resource scoped to the target Data Factory. Sets logAnalyticsDestinationType = "Dedicated" so logs land in the resource-specific ADFPipelineRun / ADFActivityRun tables (matching the bundled KQL queries) rather than the legacy AzureDiagnostics table. ExecutePipelinePolicy ADF policy shape applied to every emitted ExecutePipeline activity (#143). FailureNotificationBuilder Builds the Web + Fail activity pair that fires when an upstream activity fails (#143). The Web activity POSTs to a parameterised webhook (Logic App / Teams / PagerDuty / etc) with the failed activity's error message, and a subsequent Fail activity re-throws so the pipeline still reports failure to its caller. The Web activity has policy.secureInput/secureOutput set so the webhook URL never enters run history. FaultToleranceOptions Copy-activity fault tolerance options (#143). IncrementalCopyActivityBuilder Builds the per-mapping incremental activity group (#147). For each container → table mapping selected for incremental copy, the orchestrator inserts: LookupWatermark_<m> — Azure SQL Lookup with a literal-mapping-key SELECT against the watermark table. SetVariable_LastTs_<m> — Reads the Lookup output into lastWatermark_<m>. SetVariable_NewTs_<m> — Computes newWatermark_<m> = max(lastWatermark, unix(utcnow()) - safetyLag). The clamp prevents safety-lag arithmetic from producing a backwards window. (when validation on) LookupSrc_<c> with incremental WHERE. The Copy activity (passed in pre-built; this builder rewrites typeProperties.source.query). ScriptUpdateWatermark_<m> — Script (NonQuery) running the MERGE with backwards-protection guard, depends on Copy Succeeded. Mapping key format: <srcDb>::<srcContainer>->[<tgtDb>].[<schema>].[<table>]. This composite key prevents same-named containers across different source databases (or fan-out to multiple sink tables) from corrupting each other's watermark — see rubber-duck Blocker B5. IncrementalCopyOptions Incremental copy configuration (#147). Default Enabled is false so output is byte-identical to the post-#146 baseline unless the operator opts in. When enabled, each per-mapping Copy is wrapped in a LookupWatermark → SetVariable_LastTs → SetVariable_NewTs → [LookupSrc] → Copy → ScriptUpdateWatermark chain. The Copy's typeProperties.source.query is overridden with a WHERE c._ts > lastTs AND c._ts <= newTs predicate. The newTs is clamped to utcnow() - WatermarkSafetyLagSeconds to avoid losing documents committed within the boundary second (Cosmos _ts is second-granular). Mapping Data Flow change-feed support (the second Microsoft-documented incremental pattern, exposed as enableChangeFeed / startFromBeginning on the data-flow source) is intentionally NOT in this sub-issue. The IncrementalCopyMode.ChangeFeedDataFlow enum slot is reserved for parent #69 to add later without breaking the option surface. IncrementalGroup Per-mapping activity group produced by BuildGroup(ContainerMapping, string, string, string, string, PipelineActivity, AdfNameRegistry, IncrementalCopyOptions). Names + variables surface to the orchestrator so the per-db pipeline can declare matching parameters, variables, and (optionally) a parameter-template entry. JsonPathEscaper Builds JSONPath expressions for ADF translator mappings. Bracket-notation is used for every segment so field names containing dots, hyphens, spaces, or quotes work without manual escaping by the operator. LinkedServiceBuilder Builds the parameterised ADF linked services. Default emit mode is System-Assigned Managed Identity for both connectors (the modern recommended shape). Key Vault wiring is opt-in via UseAzureKeyVault. MonitoringOptions Monitoring & logging configuration (#144). Defaults are deployment-safe: the diagnostic-settings ARM template is emitted, every Copy activity gets monitoring-friendly userProperties, and the KQL cheat-sheet is written out so operators can drop the queries into Log Analytics on day one. ParameterCatalog Single source of truth for every ADF parameter name used by the generated artifacts. Centralising the names means linked-service builders, dataset builders, copy-activity builders, the orchestrator's adf-parameters.template.json, and the unit tests all reference identical strings — typos cause test failures, not silent runtime errors. ParameterCatalog.ParameterDefinition Typed descriptor for a single ADF parameter: the Name surfaced in generated artifact JSON, the ADF Type token (e.g. "string"), a human-readable Description, and an optional DefaultValue baked into the parameter block at generation time. SqlIdentifierEscaper Bracket-quotes T-SQL identifiers using SQL Server rules: [name], with any literal ] doubled to ]]. Centralised so #145 row-count validation queries (and any future generated T-SQL) cannot be tricked by an assessment-supplied identifier that contains ]. SqlLiteralEscaper Escapes T-SQL string literals by doubling single quotes (' → ''). Used by #147 incremental queries that bake the mapping key as a literal into a sqlReaderQuery / Script body (the alternative would be ADF Lookup parameter binding, which Azure SQL Lookup does not support without switching to SqlServerStoredProcedure). UserPropertiesBuilder Builds the ADF activity-level userProperties list that flows into the UserProperties custom-dimension column on the ADFActivityRun Log Analytics table (when the diagnostic setting uses logAnalyticsDestinationType = "Dedicated"). Centralising the shape means every Copy activity in every pipeline emits the same monitoring contract. ValidationActivityBuilder Builds the (LookupSource, LookupTarget, IfCondition) activity triplet that performs row-count parity validation around a Copy activity (#145). Pattern (per Microsoft Learn): LookupSrc → Copy → LookupTgt → IfCondition(expression) └ ifFalseActivities: [Fail] Caller is responsible for chaining the Copy activity's dependsOn to the LookupSource so it doesn't start before the baseline count is captured. ValidationActivityBuilder.ValidationTriplet Triplet returned by Build(ContainerMapping, string, string, string, AdfNameRegistry, ValidationOptions). Caller adds these to the pipeline activity list and wires the Copy activity's dependsOn to LookupSourceName. Total activity contribution (including nested Fail inside IfCondition) is 4. ValidationOptions Row-count validation configuration (#145). Defaults are conservative: validation is enabled with strict equality and zero tolerance. Operators that expect drift (e.g. mid-load Cosmos writes, soft-deleted rows) can opt down to RowCountAtLeast with a tolerance budget. WatermarkSchemaBuilder Generates the idempotent T-SQL DDL for the per-mapping watermark table that the #147 incremental copy pattern reads from / writes to. The same DDL is emitted as a stand-alone .sql file under ADF/SQL/ and (when EnsureWatermarkTableAtRuntime is on) embedded inside an ADF Script activity at the start of every per-db pipeline so the pipeline self-bootstraps the table on first run. Schema: [mappingKey] NVARCHAR(450) PRIMARY KEY — composite key chosen to fit SQL Server's 900-byte index limit (NVARCHAR(450) = 900 bytes). [lastTs] BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 — Unix seconds; matches Cosmos _ts domain. [updatedUtc] DATETIME2(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT SYSUTCDATETIME() — audit column; not used by the runtime but useful for ops. Structs CopyActivityBuilder.BuildResult Pairs the Copy activity produced by Build(ContainerMapping, string, string, SinkWriteBehavior, AdfNameRegistry, DataFactoryGenerationOptions?) with any warnings surfaced during translator-mapping construction (e.g. skipped transformed fields, deferred child tables). FailureNotificationBuilder.NotificationPair Pairs the Web (webhook POST) and Fail (re-throw) activities emitted by Build(PipelineActivity, string, AdfNameRegistry) for a single upstream activity failure. Interfaces IDataFactoryPipelineGenerator Generates ready-to-deploy Azure Data Factory artifacts (linked services, datasets, pipelines) from an AssessmentResult. Foundational sub-issue #141 of parent #70: copy activities per container → table mapping. Later sub-issues extend the output with parameters, retry policy, monitoring, validation, ARM templating, and incremental-load support. Enums IncrementalCopyMode Incremental copy implementation strategy (#147). Only TimestampWatermark is implemented in this sub-issue. The ChangeFeedDataFlow slot is reserved for parent #69 to add a Mapping-Data-Flow-based change-feed implementation alongside, without breaking the public option surface. SinkWriteBehavior ADF Copy-activity sink write behaviour controlling whether rows are inserted fresh or upserted using target-table key columns. ValidationStrategy Row-count validation comparison strategy (#145).

reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.summary: Class AnomalyDetectionService Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.Monitoring Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Detects anomalies in migration Request-Units/sec and throughput using a simple rolling-window z-score baseline (#226). Feeds from the enriched MigrationProgressSnapshot stream produced by MigrationMonitoringService and is surfaced as warnings by the migration status command (#225). public sealed class AnomalyDetectionService Inheritance object AnomalyDetectionService Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The detector keeps a fixed-length window of recent observed values per (pipeline, run, activity, metric) key. For each new observation it evaluates the value against the baseline formed by the prior values in the window (so a value never contaminates its own baseline), then appends it. This type is stateful and not thread-safe. It is intended to be driven by a single streaming loop (the scoped CLI run). Do not share one instance across concurrent consumers. The fixed sample-count window is a heuristic and does not model elapsed time or migration phase changes; tune ZScoreThreshold and MinRelativeChange if false positives occur. Constructors AnomalyDetectionService(AnomalyDetectionOptions, ILogger<AnomalyDetectionService>) Creates the anomaly detection service. public AnomalyDetectionService(AnomalyDetectionOptions options, ILogger<AnomalyDetectionService> logger) Parameters options AnomalyDetectionOptions Detector configuration. logger ILogger<AnomalyDetectionService> Logger for diagnostics. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Any argument is null. ArgumentException options contains an invalid combination. Updates internal per-key window state as a side effect. public IReadOnlyList<MigrationAnomaly> Detect(MigrationProgressSnapshot snapshot) Parameters snapshot MigrationProgressSnapshot The progress snapshot to evaluate. reference/CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.summary: Class AzureMonitorMetricPayloadBuilder Namespace CosmosToSqlAssessment.Services.Monitoring Assembly CosmosToSqlAssessment.dll Builds Azure Monitor custom-metric ingestion payloads from MigrationMetricPoints. The output matches the documented schema: { "time": "<ISO-8601 UTC>", "data": { "baseData": { "metric": "...", "namespace": "...", "dimNames": ["..."], "series": [ { "dimValues": ["..."], "min": n, "max": n, "sum": n, "count": n } ] } } } Points that share a namespace, metric name, timestamp, and dimension-name shape are grouped into a single payload with one series per point. This keeps each payload valid (Azure Monitor requires a stable dimNames array across all series in a payload). The class is pure and deterministic, so it is exercised directly in unit tests without a live call. public sealed class AzureMonitorMetricPayloadBuilder Inheritance object AzureMonitorMetricPayloadBuilder Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods BuildPayloadJson(IEnumerable<MigrationMetricPoint>?) Returns IReadOnlyList<string> One JSON string per payload group. BuildPayloads(IEnumerable<MigrationMetricPoint>?)
